clean_story:
Facts without filters. Reality unshaped by wish.

The_truth is the raw data of existence—what remains when opinions, fears, and stories are stripped away. Like a mirror that shows cracks in the glass instead of smoothing them, truth doesn’t adjust for comfort.

Here’s how it works: A lie is a shortcut, like drawing a map without mountains. Truth includes the terrain, even if it slows you down. Example: Water boils at 100 Celsius at sea level—not because we agree on it, but because molecules behave that way. Change the altitude, and the number shifts; the mechanism stays. Truth resists bending.

Yet humans often prefer "useful" versions. A compass needle points north, but sailors once called magnetic north true north—until they discovered the difference. The correction didn’t erase the old term; it revealed a hidden layer.

Truth isn’t always accessible. Light takes time to reach eyes; memories decay; instruments have limits. But its test is simple: If every observer with the same tools sees the same result, you’re close.

Three related subjects are perception versus reality, scientific measurement limits, and why lies spread faster.
